{{Short description|2023 Italian film festival event}} {{EngvarB|date=July 2023}} {{Use dmy dates|date=July 2023}} {{Infobox film festival|name=80th Venice International Film Festival|image=80th Venice Film Festival poster.jpg|caption=2023 official poster by [[Lorenzo Mattotti]]|opening=''[[Comandante (2023 film)|Comandante]]''|closing=''[[Society of the Snow]]''|location=[[Venice]], [[Italy]]|founded=1932|awards=[[Golden Lion]]:
''[[Poor Things (film)|Poor Things]]''|host=[[Caterina Murino]]|date=30 August – 9 September 2023|website={{URL|https://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/2023}}|chronology=Venice Film Festival|preceded_by=[[79th Venice International Film Festival|79th]]|followed_by=[[81st Venice International Film Festival|81st]]|artistic_director=[[Alberto Barbera]]}} The '''80th annual [[Venice Film Festival|Venice International Film Festival]]''' was held from 30 August to 9 September 2023, at [[Lido di Venezia|Venice Lido]] in [[Italy]]. [[Alberto Barbera]], Venice's artistic director, acknowledged that the [[2023 SAG-AFTRA strike]] was likely to impact the festival if it was not resolved in time, due to the strike rules that prevent all actors from taking part in any kind of promotional activities for their films. Nevertheless, the festival proceeded though fewer Hollywood celebrities than usual were in attendance, while an increase of European titles in all sections was expected. The world premieres of some upcoming award season hot titles expected to premiere at Venice, such as [[Coen brothers|Ethan Coen]]'s [[Drive-Away Dolls|''Drive-Away-Dolls'']], ended up delayed.{{Cite web |date=2023-07-18 |title=Venice Film Festival preparing Plan B if Hollywood strikes continue |url=https://www.euronews.com/culture/2023/07/18/venice-preparing-pan-european-festival-amid-potential-hollywood-strike-impact-on-fall-fest |access-date=2023-07-20 |website=euronews |language=en}} As the strike was ongoing, some American films announced the absence of the cast in the red carpet of their world premieres, including [[Bradley Cooper]], director/writer/producer and actor of [[Maestro (2023 film)|''Maestro'']].{{Cite web |last=Mendelson |first=Scott |date=2023-07-31 |title=Exclusive: Bradley Cooper Skips Venice Premiere of 'Maestro' in Solidarity With SAG-AFTRA Strike |url=https://www.thewrap.com/bradley-cooper-maestro-premiere-venice/ |access-date=2023-08-17 |website=TheWrap |language=en-US}} American filmmaker [[Damien Chazelle]] served as Jury President for the main competition. Italian actress [[Caterina Murino]] hosted the opening and closing nights of the festival. The [[Golden Lion]] was awarded to ''[[Poor Things (film)|Poor Things]]'' by [[Yorgos Lanthimos]].{{cite web |title=Venice Film Festival Awards Winners Announced – Live |url=https://deadline.com/2023/09/venice-film-festival-2023-awards-winners-full-list-1235541624/ |publisher=deadline}} Italian director [[Liliana Cavani]] and Chinese/Hong Kong actor [[Tony Leung Chiu-wai]] were both awarded with the [[Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement]] during the festival.{{Cite web |date=2023-03-27 |title=Biennale Cinema 2023 {{!}} Director Liliana Cavani and actor Tony Leung Chiu-wai Golden Lions for Lifetime Achievement |url=https://www.labiennale.org/en/news/director-liliana-cavani-and-actor-tony-leung-chiu-wai-golden-lions-lifetime-achievement |access-date=2023-07-25 |website=La Biennale di Venezia |language=en}}{{cite web |title=LIVE: The awards of the 80th Venice Film Festival |url=https://cineuropa.org/en/newsdetail/449623 |publisher=cineeupora}} The festival opened with ''[[Comandante (2023 film)|Comandante]]'' by [[Edoardo De Angelis]].{{Cite web |date=2023-07-21 |title=Biennale Cinema 2023 {{!}} Edoardo De Angelis’ Comandante is the new opening film of the Biennale Cinema 2023 |url=https://www.labiennale.org/en/news/edoardo-de-angelis%E2%80%99-comandante-new-opening-film-biennale-cinema-2023 |access-date=2023-07-21 |website=La Biennale di Venezia |language=en}} [[Luca Guadagnino]]'s ''[[Challengers (film)|Challengers]]'' was originally scheduled to have its world premiere as the The section usually also presents a selection of documentaries about cinema. Director and screenwriter [[Andrea Pallaoro]] will chair a Jury of Film Students which will award the Venice Classics prizes for the respective competitions for ''Best Restored Film'' and for the ''Best Documentary About Cinema''. The Jury chaired will be composed of 24 students, each of them recommended by professors of film studies from various Italian universities. ''Final Cut in Venice'' is the festival program that has been providing since 2013 support in the completion of films from African and Middle East countries. The following seven work-in-progress films have been selected for the 11th edition of Final Cut in Venice:{{Cite web |date=2023-07-14 |title=Biennale Cinema 2023 {{!}} Final Cut in Venice: 7 work-in-progress films have been selected |url=https://www.labiennale.org/en/news/final-cut-venice-7-work-progress-films-have-been-selected |access-date=2023-07-16 |website=La Biennale di Venezia |language=en}} {| class="wikitable" !English title !Original title !Director(s) !Production country |- ! colspan="4" |Fiction |- |''Allah is Not Obliged'' |''Allah n'est pas obligé'' |Zaven Najjar |France, Luxembourg, Belgium, Saudi Arabia, Canada, Slovakia, United States |- | colspan="2" |''[[Carissa (film)|Carissa]]'' |Jason Jacobs and Devon Delmar |South Africa |- | colspan="2" |''Happy Holidays'' |[[Scandar Copti]] |Palestine, Germany, France, Qatar, Italy |- |''Life Is a Railroad'' |''La Vie est un chemin de fer'' |Kevin Mavakala, Manassé Kashala, Tousmy Kilo, Isaac Sahani |Democratic Republic of Congo, France, Germany |- ! colspan="4" |Documentary |- |''Sudan, When Poems Fall Apart'' |''Soudan retiens les chants qui s'effondrent'' |[[Hind Meddeb]] |France, Tunisia |- | colspan="2" |''She Was Not Alone'' |Hussein Al-Asadi |Iraq, Saudi Arabia |- | colspan="2" |''Zion Music'' |[[Rama Thiaw]] |Senegal, Ivory Coast, Germany |} == Independent sections == === Venice International Critics’ Week (''38. Settimana Internazionale della Critica'') === The titles in competition will compete for the €5,000 Grand Prize and the €3,000 Audience Award. The selection committee is led by delegate general Beatrice Fiorentino with members including Enrico Azzano, Chiara Borroni, Ilaria Feole, and Federico Pedroni. ''[[Green Border]]'' by [[Agnieszka Holland]] == Controversy == During the Festival, casting director of ''[[Io capitano]],'' Henri-Didier Njikam, was denied an entry visa by the [[List of diplomatic missions in Morocco|Italian Embassy in Rabat]], [[Morocco]], because there were no guarantees that he would leave Italian territory once he entered. In an interview with ''[[The Hollywood Reporter Roma]]'', Njikam said the fact was perceived as "an act of racism" as "the embassy justified the refusal by claiming that there were no guarantees that I would leave Italian territory once I entered, in Venice. They basically treated me as a migrant, as if I wanted to take advantage of the situation to escape. But I have a job, a professional card from the Moroccan Film Center. And, frankly, if I wanted to work in Europe, I would have already done so," and that the entity "did not look at my resume or my documents, only the color of my skin. [...] This problem exists only with the Italian embassy in Morocco, because my colleagues from [[Ghana]] and the [[Ivory Coast]] were able to leave. If I had been white, I don't think I would have been treated this way."{{Cite web |last=Ravarino |first=Ilaria |date=September 6, 2023 |title=Il direttore casting di Io, Capitano: "Visto negato, umiliato dall'Italia" |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.it/film/festival-e-premi/e-nel-garrone-day-il-suo-direttore-del-casting-rimane-bloccato-in-marocco-litalia-mi-ha-negato-il-visto-perche-sono-camerunense/45304/ |access-date=September 11, 2023 |website=[[The Hollywood Reporter Roma]] |language=it}} == References == {{reflist}} ==External links== * {{official website|https://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/2023}} {{Venice Film Festival}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Venice Film Festival}} [[Category:2023 film festivals]] [[Category:2023 in Italian cinema]] [[Category:Venice Film Festival|80]] [[Category:2020s in Venice|Film]] [[Category:August 2023 in Italy]] [[Category:September 2023 in Italy]]
